title: Scalable Diffusion Models with Transformers
abstract: We explore a new class of diffusion models based on the transformer architecture. We train latent diffusion models of images, replacing the commonly-used U-Net backbone with a transformer that operates on latent patches. We analyze the scalability of our Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) through the lens of forward pass complexity as measured by Gflops. We find that DiTs with higher Gflops consistently have lower FID. Our largest model, DiT-XL/2, achieves a state-of-the-art FID of 2.27 on class-conditional ImageNet-256.

title: DINOv3: Self-Supervised Visual Representation Learning with Multi-Scale Vision Transformers
abstract: We present DINOv3, a self-supervised method for training visual features using multi-scale vision transformers. Building on the DINO self-distillation framework, DINOv3 introduces cross-scale attention mechanisms that enable the model to learn representations capturing both local detail and global context. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art results on image classification, segmentation, and detection benchmarks, producing universal visual features that transfer effectively across tasks without fine-tuning.
